title: China Telecom Deploys 2,682-Photon 'Tianyan-P2000' Web Platform to Achieve Dual-Modality Quantum Advantage Services - Quantum Computing Report
gist: China Telecom Quantum Group put the Jiuzhang-4.0-derived, 2,682-photon Tianyan-P2000 photonic quantum computer into production on its public Tianyan cloud, claiming a 29-microsecond boson-sampling task versus an estimated 16-billion-year classical runtime, and creating the first multi-tenant cloud offering both photonic and superconducting quantum-advantage access across 60 countries.

themes.theme: Dual-modality cloud as a competitive first

insights.claim: The Tianyan-P2000 integration establishes what is claimed to be the world's first multi-tenant network fabric delivering verified quantum computational advantage through both photonic and superconducting physical modalities simultaneously.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A dual-modality commercial cloud is a distinct competitive positioning versus IBM/Google (superconducting-only) or photonic-pure-play efforts — relevant for assessing which national/corporate quantum cloud ecosystem is pulling ahead on breadth of commercial access.
insights.quote: the integration establishes the world’s first multi-tenant network fabric capable of delivering verified quantum computational advantage through both photonic and superconducting physical modalities.

insights.claim: China Telecom's Tianyan cloud now operates six public cloud-connected quantum processors total (five superconducting QPUs plus the new photonic node), and has processed over 4 million experimental tasks with more than 50 million visits to date.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Concrete usage metrics (4M+ tasks, 50M+ visits) indicate this is a genuinely utilized commercial platform, not a demo — a scale benchmark to compare against IBM Quantum Network or other cloud quantum offerings.
insights.quote: the integrated Tianyan cloud platform has processed over 4 million individual experimental tasks and recorded more than 50 million visits
insights.locator: The onboarding of this photonic node

insights.claim: The Tianyan-P2000 node is actively routing production resources to clients across 60 countries and regions after a rigorous technical and security vetting process for administrative access.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: International reach (60 countries) despite a security vetting gate suggests China is positioning its quantum cloud as a geopolitically significant export/soft-power platform, not a domestic-only tool — worth watching for export-control or access-restriction responses from Western governments.
insights.quote: the Tianyan-P2000 node is actively routing production resources to international universities, industrial research laboratories, and commercial clients across 60 countries and regions.
insights.locator: Following its onboarding to the network

themes.theme: Photonic architecture and claimed quantum advantage

insights.claim: The Tianyan-P2000 is built directly on the Jiuzhang 4.0 optical architecture (published in Nature in May 2026) and manipulates 2,682 distinct photons simultaneously through an interferometric waveguide mesh, engineered by a team under control specialist Liu Chunwang in partnership with Jiuzhang (Jinan) Quantum Technology Co. and CAS.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Shows China moving research-grade photonic breakthroughs (Jiuzhang 4.0) into commercial production within months of Nature publication — a fast lab-to-cloud pipeline that's a leading indicator for how quickly China commercializes quantum advances.
insights.quote: The physical construction of the Tianyan-P2000 is based directly on the core optical architecture of Jiuzhang 4.0, a milestone photonic quantum computing prototype developed by the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC) whose performance metrics were published in Nature in May.
insights.locator: Replicating the Jiuzhang 4.0 Multiphoton Architectural Blueprint

insights.claim: In a formal benchmark, Tianyan-P2000 executed a multi-photon boson sampling computation in 29 microseconds, versus an estimated 16 billion years for a classical supercomputer to replicate the same workload — demonstrating absolute computational advantage for this narrow problem class.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A concrete, extreme quantum-advantage figure (29 microseconds vs. 16 billion years) is a headline claim investors should track for independent verification/challenge, since boson sampling advantage claims have historically been contested by classical algorithm improvements.
insights.quote: Tianyan-P2000 Processing —► 29 Microseconds (Natively executed via non-linear light interference) Classical Supercomputing —► 16 Billion Years (Estimated calculation run-time for a classical emulator)
insights.locator: Cloud Post-Selection Workflows for Graph Analysis and Molecular Modeling

insights.claim: Photonic quantum processors operate at ambient room temperature (unlike superconducting systems needing 10-20 mK cryogenics), giving them lower noise floors from electromagnetic/thermal immunity, extended coherence storage, and native interoperability with existing telecom fiber-optic and QKD infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Room-temperature operation and native fiber compatibility could make photonic quantum processors cheaper to operate at data-center scale and easier to network into quantum communication infrastructure than superconducting rivals — a structural cost/deployment advantage to weigh against superconducting roadmaps.
insights.quote: Unlike solid-state superconducting layouts that require deep sub-kelvin cryogenics (10–20 mK) to preserve electronic phase coherence, the photonic processor operates under ambient room-temperature conditions.

insights.claim: China Telecom has launched four commercial application verticals on the platform: graph data analysis (traffic/routing optimization), biochemical drug discovery (molecular bond simulation), spectral computation (materials science), and machine vision (non-linear image classification).
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Specific named verticals indicate where China Telecom expects near-term monetizable use cases for photonic quantum advantage — useful for benchmarking against Western quantum cloud providers' stated commercial roadmaps.
insights.quote: Once approved, developers can deploy algorithms across four launched vertical domains
insights.locator: Once approved, developers can deploy algorithms

insights.claim: This deployment builds on China Telecom's earlier 504-qubit superconducting Tianyan-504 cluster brought online in 2024, showing a multi-year, multi-modality scaling strategy rather than a single-shot announcement.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Establishes a timeline (2024 superconducting scale-up to 2026 photonic addition) showing sustained, compounding investment — useful context for projecting China Telecom's future cloud capacity roadmap.
insights.quote: It builds upon previous platform expansions, such as the 504-qubit superconducting Tianyan-504 cluster brought online in 2024.
insights.locator: Developed in partnership with Jiuzhang (Jinan) Quantum Technology Co.
